title: Result - Ireland
content:
Welcome to Ireland, a warm and welcoming island known for its stunning coastal scenery, rich history and vibrant culture. Explore County Donegal, named the "Coolest Place on the Planet" by National Geographic and marvel at the towering Slieve League Cliffs.
Drive the Wild Atlantic Way, the world’s longest coastal route at 2,500 kilometres, or delve into 5,000 years of history in Ireland’s Ancient East. With Irish (Gaeilge) and English as its official languages, Ireland thrives as the "land of saints and scholars." Boasting a skilled, multicultural workforce and a competitive economy, Ireland offers endless opportunities and charm.

## Unique food tradition
Brown sodabread: a dense, crumbly and tangy quick bread that's leavened with baking soda and buttermilk, instead of yeast.
Ireland has a growing economy that is one of the most competitive in the world and offers new employment opportunities in a range of sectors.

### Local legend or myth
Children of Lir: 4 siblings in Irish mythology who are transformed into swans by their jealous stepmother. They are cursed to spend 900 years in the form of birds.

### Language of the Land
The official languages of the Republic of Ireland are English and Irish (Gaeilge); however, English is more widely spoken and very few people outside of rural areas speak Irish as their first language.
snakes are found in Ireland, a fact that has inspired the legend of St. Patrick driving all the snakes into the sea.
Ready to make your plans to work and travel abroad? Take the first step by learning more about your selected country and choose the work permit that's right for you.
